output:
Here's a simple and affordable 7-day meal plan tailored to your requirements and preferences:

Day 1:
Breakfast: Gluten-free oatmeal with banana and cinnamon
Lunch: Grilled chicken breast with roasted potatoes and steamed broccoli
Dinner: Thai-inspired coconut curry with chicken and vegetables (no onion) served with rice

Day 2:
Breakfast: Scrambled eggs with spinach and gluten-free toast
Lunch: Taco salad with ground beef, lettuce, tomatoes, and corn (no cheese)
Dinner: Baked salmon with sweet potato mash and green beans

Day 3:
Breakfast: Greek yogurt (lactose-free) with berries and gluten-free granola
Lunch: Turkey and avocado wrap using gluten-free tortilla
Dinner: Indian-style chicken tikka (without cream) with basmati rice and cucumber salad

Day 4:
Breakfast: Smoothie bowl with almond milk, banana, spinach, and chia seeds
Lunch: Quinoa salad with grilled vegetables and chickpeas
Dinner: Pork chops with apple sauce, mashed potatoes, and steamed carrots

Day 5:
Breakfast: Gluten-free pancakes with maple syrup and fresh fruit
Lunch: Chicken and vegetable soup (no onion) with gluten-free crackers
Dinner: Mexican-style fajitas with beef, bell peppers, and corn tortillas (gluten-free)

Day 6:
Breakfast: Bacon and eggs with gluten-free toast and sliced tomatoes
Lunch: Baked potato topped with chili con carne (no onion)
Dinner: Grilled steak with roasted vegetables and herb-infused olive oil

Day 7:
Breakfast: Fruit salad with gluten-free muesli and almond milk
Lunch: Leftover fajitas or curry from previous meals
Dinner: Roast chicken with rosemary potatoes and steamed mixed vegetables

Notes:
1. Use lactose-free milk and yogurt where dairy is involved.
2. Ensure all products are gluten-free, including sauces and condiments.
3. Avoid using onions in all recipes.
4. Adjust spice levels to accommodate your super-tasting gene.
5. Use herbs and mild spices for flavor instead of excessive salt or sugar.
6. Feel free to swap meals between days based on your preference or leftover situation.

This meal plan focuses on your likes (meat, potatoes, Mexican, Thai, and Indian cuisines) while avoiding allergens and intolerances. It's designed to be simple and cost-effective, with some meals creating leftovers that can be used later in the week.
